title: dm20 MCP query cache (Phase 16)
description: Cache effectiveness for the dm20 MCP allow-listed tool surface. Hit rate measures how often the SmartMonsterDriver and gameplay layer re-use a cached MCP response within TTL instead of crossing the HTTP boundary. Sizes track L1 in-process LRU and L2 SQLite footprint. Invalidations spike on dm20 schema-version bumps (auto via the poller) and on operator-issued invalidate() calls.

metrics.name: hit_rate
metrics.healthy_range: 30-70% hit rate during active combat (frequent rules lookups)

metrics.name: size
metrics.healthy_range: L1 ≤ MCPCACHE_L1_SIZE (default 512); L2 = -1 when disabled, else growing toward TTL steady-state

metrics.name: invalidations_total
metrics.healthy_range: Low baseline; spikes correlate with dm20 schema bumps or operator invalidate() calls

query_recipe: Phoenix UI → Project 'eldritch-mcp-cache' → Three panels: (1) Filter span_name='eldritch.mcp.cache', plot (count where layer IN [l1_hit,l2_hit]) / total grouped by eldritch.mcp.tool_name over 1h. (2) Filter span_name='eldritch.mcp.cache', plot last(eldritch.mcp.cache.size_l1) and last(eldritch.mcp.cache.size_l2) over 5m. (3) Filter span_name='eldritch.mcp.cache.invalidation', plot sum(eldritch.mcp.cache.invalidation.entries_removed) grouped by scope over 24h.
